verses.sanskrit: कृत आतिथ्योऽथ रामस्तु सूर्यस्य उदयनम् प्रति ।आमंत्र्य स मुनीम् तत् सर्वान् वनम् एव अन्वगाहत ॥३-२-१॥
verses.text:
Then, on receiving the hospitality in that hermitage for that day, and on the next
  day before sunrise, Rama bade farewell to all of the sages, and thus entered the deep forests.
  [3-2-1]

verses.sanskrit: नाना मृग गण आकीर्णम् ऋक्ष शार्दूल सेवितम् ।ध्वस्त वृक्ष लता गुल्मम् दुर्दर्श सलिलाशयम् ॥३-२-२॥
verses.text:
Rama followed by Lakshmana has indeed seen that midst of the forest which is
  filled with very many animal herds, where the bears and tigers are frequenting, and whose trees,
  creepers, bushes are ruined, water ponds rendered unsightly, birds song-less, but where the
  swarms of crickets are whistling deafeningly. [3-3-2, 3]

verses.sanskrit: निष्कूजमाना शकुनि झिल्लिका गण नादितम् ।लक्ष्मण अनुचरोओ रामो वन मध्यम् ददर्श ह ॥३-२-३॥
सीताया सह काकुत्स्थः तस्मिन् घोर मृग आयुते ।ददर्श गिरि शृङ्ग आभम् पुरुषादम् महास्वनम् ॥३-२-४॥
verses.text:
Therein Rama has seen along with Seetha, a lurid voiced man-eater who is akin to a
  mountain-peak, among horrendous animals. [3-2-4]

verses.sanskrit: गम्भीर अक्षम् महावक्त्रम् विकटम् विकटोदरम् ।बीभत्सम् विषमम् दीर्घम् विकृतम् घोर दर्शनम् ॥३-२-५॥
verses.text:
Deep eyed, huge mouthed, horrible with a monstrous belly, hideously misshapen, and
  a very soaring one that demon is, an ugly one with a horrendous look. [3-2-5]

verses.sanskrit: वसानम् चर्म  वैयाघ्रम् वस आर्द्रम् रुधिरोक्षितम् ।त्रासनम् सर्व भूतानाम् व्यादितास्यम् इव अन्तकम् ॥३-२-६॥
verses.text:
Wearing tiger's skin that is wetted with fat and dampened with blood, he is
  frightening to all beings like the wide-open mouth of Death. [3-2-6]

verses.sanskrit: त्रीन् सिंहान् चतुरो व्याघ्रान् द्वौ वृकौ पृषतान् दश ।सविषाणम् वसादिग्धम् गजस्य च शिरो महत् ॥३-२-७॥
verses.text:
He skewered three lions, four tigers, two wolves, ten spotted deer, and a big head
  of an elephant with tusks and fouled with fat on an iron spear, and he is yelling with his
  deafening voice. [3-2-7-8a]

verses.sanskrit: अवसज्य आअयसे शूले विनदन्तम् महास्वनम् ।स रामम् लक्ष्मणम् चैव सीताम् दृष्ट्वा च मैथिलीम् ॥३-२-८॥
verses.text:
He that demon on seeing Rama, Lakshmana and Seetha ran towards them very angrily,
  like the Time, the Eliminator of people. [3-2-8b-9a]

verses.sanskrit: अभ्य धावत् सुसंक्रुद्धो प्रजाः काल इव अन्तकः ।स कृत्वा भैरवम् नादम् चालयन् इव मेदिनीम् ॥३-२-९॥
verses.text:
He shouted a tremulous blare, as though to tremor the earth, and seized Vaidehi
  with his arms and gone afar, and then said this way. [9b-10a]

verses.sanskrit: अङ्केन आदाय वैदेहीम् अपक्रम्य तदा अब्रवीत् ।युवाम् जटा चीर धरौ सभार्यौ क्षीण जीवितौ ॥३-२-१०॥
verses.text:
"You two wear jute cloths like hermits, but handle arrows, bows and swords like
  warriors, yet entered Dandaka forest with your wife... your lives are on the wane...
  [3-2-10b-11a]

verses.sanskrit: प्रविष्टौ दण्डकारण्यम् शर चाप असि पाणिनौ ।कथम् तापसयोः युवाम् च वासः प्रमदया सह ॥३-२-११॥
verses.text:
"How saintly are you while living with your wife? You sinful fellows dishonest are
  you ways... who are you... you insulters of sainthood? [3-2-11b-12a]

verses.sanskrit: अधर्म चारिणौ पापौ कौ युवाम् मुनि दूषकौ ।अहम् वनम् इदम् दुर्गम् विराघो नाम राक्षसः ॥३-२-१२॥
verses.text:
"I am a demon named Viradha and I will be on the rove in this impassable forest
  with weapon, always eating the flesh of sages... [3-2-12b-13a]

verses.sanskrit: चरामि सायुधो नित्यम् ऋषि मांसानि भक्षयन् ।इयम् नारी वरारोहा मम भार्या भविष्यति ॥३-२-१३॥
verses.text:
"This best waisted woman will be my wife, and I will drink your blood of you two
  sinful one in a fight with you... [3-2-13b-14a]

verses.sanskrit: युवयोः पापयोः च अहम् पास्यामि रुधिरम् मृधे ।तस्य एवम् ब्रुवतो दुष्टम् विराधस्य दुरात्मनः ॥३-२-१४॥
verses.text:
On listening those arrogant words spoken in bad intent by that evil minded
  Viradha, Seetha, the daughter of Janaka, is much panicked. [3-2-14b-15a.]

verses.sanskrit: श्रुत्वा सगर्वितम् वाक्यम् संभ्रान्ता जनकात्मजा ।सीता प्रावेपिता उद्वेगात् प्रवाते कदली यथा ॥३-२-१५॥
verses.text:
Seetha in fear wiggled like a plantain tree in a whirlwind, and on seeing at the
  auspicious lady gone into the arms of Viradha, Raghava spoke this sentence to Lakshmana, with his
  face fully turning to pale. [3-2-15b-16]

verses.sanskrit: ताम् दृष्ट्वा राघवः सीताम् विराध अङ्कगताम् शुभाम् ।अब्रवीत् लक्ष्मणम् वाक्यम् मुखेन परिशुष्यता ॥३-२-१६॥
पश्य सौम्य नरेन्द्रस्य जनकस्य अत्म संभवाम् ।मम भार्याम् शुभाचाराम् विराधाङ्के प्रवेशिताम् ॥३-२-१७॥
verses.text:
"See! Gentle Lakshmana, see that illustrious princes Seetha, the daughter of
  emperor Janaka, one who is brought up comfortably in immense comfort, and my auspiciously
  traditional wife... now gone into the wretched hands of a demon... [3-2-17-18a]

verses.sanskrit: अत्यन्त सुख संवृद्धाम् राजपुत्रीम् यशस्विनीम् ।यत् अभिप्रेतम् अस्मासु प्रियम् वर वृतम् च यत् ॥३-२-१८॥
verses.text:
"That which is desired to befall on us, and that which is the choicest desire of
  Kaikeyi, and that which is concomitant to her boons, oh, Lakshmana, that has come to pass
  quickly, and today only Lakshmana... [3-2-18b-19a]

verses.sanskrit: कैकेय्यास्तु सुसंवृत्तम् क्षिप्रम् अद्य एव लक्ष्मण ।या न तुष्यति राज्येन पुत्रार्थे दीर्घ दर्शिनी ॥३-२-१९॥
verses.text:
"She who is a foresighted lady, she who is not happy with the kingdom for her son,
  she by whom I, a dear one to all beings, am sent to forests, and she is my middle mother, her
  desire is fulfilled today, nay now itself... [3-2-19b-20]

verses.sanskrit: ययाऽहम् सर्वभूतानाम् प्रियः प्रस्थापितो वनम् ।अद्य इदानीम् सकामा सा या माता मम मध्यमा ॥३-२-२०॥
पर स्पर्शात् तु वैदेह्या न दुःखतरम् अस्ति मे ।पितुर् विनाशात् सौमित्रे स्व राज्य हरणात् तथा ॥३-२-२१॥
verses.text:
"To me, oh, Soumitri, the grief of others touching Seetha is more, than father's
  demise or grabbing away my kingdom... [3-2-21]

verses.sanskrit: इति ब्रुवति काकुत्स्थे बाष्प शोक परिप्लुतः ।अब्रवीत् लक्ष्मणः क्रुद्धो रुद्धो नाग इव श्वसन् ॥३-२-२२॥
verses.text:
When said thus by Rama, Lakshmana said this with flooding tears of lament, and in
  his anger hissing like a forestalled snake. [3-2-22]

verses.sanskrit: अनाथ इव भूतानाम् नाथः त्वम् वासवोपमः ।मया प्रेष्येण काकुत्स्थः किम् अर्थम् परितप्यसे ॥३-२-२३॥
verses.text:
"Oh, Rama, you being the Indra like lord of all beings, and while you are being
  attended by an adherent like me, what for you worry yourself like a derelict? [3-2-23]

verses.sanskrit: शरेण निहतस्य अद्य मया क्रुद्धेन रक्षसः ।विराधस्य गत असोः हि मही पास्यति शोणितम् ॥३-२-२४॥
verses.text:
"He will now be felled with my arrow as I am enraged at him, and gone are his
  lives now, and indeed the earth shall drink his blood... [3-2-24]

verses.sanskrit: राज्य कामे मम क्रोधो भरते यो बभूव ह ।तम् विराधे विमोक्ष्यामि वज्री वज्रम् इव अचले ॥३-२-२५॥
verses.text:
"Indeed, that which anger was there to me on Bharata when he desired kingdom, that
  anger I will now release on Viradha, as Indra released his Thunderbolt on the mountain...
  [3-2-25]

verses.sanskrit: मम भुज बल वेग वेगितःपततु शरोऽस्य महान् महोरसि ।व्यपनयतु तनोः च जीवितम्पततु ततः च महीम् विघूर्णितः ॥३-२-२६॥
verses.text:
"Accelerated by my shoulder strength this ruinous arrow shall fall on his massive
  chest, thus life from his body shall decamp, and then he shall whirlingly fall onto the earth..."
  So said Lakshmana. [3-2-26] The forest
  Dandaka Forest wherein Rama is trekking is a dominion of their kingdom, but a deserted forest.
  One named Danda, the last son of Ikshvaku, was performing all demonic activities in their
  kingdom. His father Ikshvaku received many a complaint about Danda's deeds from his subjects, and
  angered at his erring son Danda and banishes him from the kingdom. Danda took refuge
  in Vindhya mountain range and built a kingdom and a beautiful capital for himself called
  Madhumanta. He rubbed shoulders with all the demons thereabout and thus became a disciple of Sage
  Shukraachaarya, the guru of demons. One day when Sage Shukraachaarya is not in hermitage, Danda
  reaches there, and sees Araja, the elder daughter of the sage Shukra, and molests her, in spite
  of her repeated protests. Later when Sage Shukraachaarya comes to know about it, gets enraged and
  curses Danda, to fall down along with his entire kingdom under a mud storm around that Madhumanta
  capital for a period of seven consecutive days. Then there is a
  mud storm for seven days and entire kingdom went under mud, later to become a forest called
  Dandaka. The place to where the curse fearing people fled from that Madhunata to further south is
  called Jansthaana. At later time, when Rama's peregrination started, these two places, Dandaka
  forest and Janasthaana, are under the domain of Ravana, and Ravana made one demon named Khara, as
  the protector of this dominion Jansthaana. This is the place
  where Rama built his hermitage Panchavati, at which Demoness Surpanakha arrives, and from where
  Ravana abducts Seetha. When Rama asks the forest dwelling sages for a quiet place to live on, the
  sages in Dandaka forest, will make Rama constantly move southward, till he reaches Janasthana,
  indirectly directing Rama to eradicate the demonic influence over these places, which once
  belonged to Rama's kingdom.
